title: rehmannia glutinosa var. typica
intro: Rehmannia glutinosa var. typica, also known as Chinese foxglove or di Huang, is a perennial plant native to Asia, belonging to the saxifrage family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and hemostatic effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as improving kidney function, nourishing the yin, and reducing blood pressure. Herbal preparations of Rehmannia glutinosa var. typica include dried roots, which are often used in teas, capsules, or decoctions, and are also combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
